topic: ChatGPT prompting strategies and real-world use cases

entries.a: Prompts.chat is the world's largest open-source prompt library for AI, designed to work with various AI models including ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Llama, and Mistral.

entries.q: How can I contribute to prompts.chat?
entries.a: You can contribute by adding prompts at prompts.chat/prompts/new, where they will sync automatically to the library.

entries.q: What is the Interactive Book of Prompting?
entries.a: The Interactive Book of Prompting is a free, interactive guide that covers over 25 chapters on prompt engineering, including basic and advanced techniques like chain-of-thought reasoning and few-shot learning.

entries.q: What is the purpose of the 'Prompting for Kids' section?
entries.a: The 'Prompting for Kids' section offers an interactive, game-based adventure designed to teach children aged 8-14 how to communicate with AI through fun puzzles and stories.

entries.q: How can I self-host my own prompt library?
entries.a: You can self-host your own prompt library by using the command 'npx prompts.chat new my-prompt-library' followed by 'cd my-prompt-library'. A setup wizard will help configure branding, theme, and authentication.

entries.q: What integrations are available for prompts.chat?
entries.a: Prompts.chat can be integrated with a CLI using 'npx prompts.chat', and it also supports plugins for Claude and can be used as an MCP server in AI tools.
Prompts.chat has been featured in Forbes, referenced by Harvard and Columbia, and has over 40 academic citations, along with being the most liked dataset on Hugging Face.
